title: Portable no-design-system implementation authority
summary: A JudgmentKit-native implementation authority for UI generation when no external design system is present, with concrete primitives, React+Tailwind recipes, accessibility rules, state coverage, and handoff requirements.

authority.when_to_use: Use this pack when the repo, prompt, and brief do not provide an authoritative external design system or when the task explicitly asks for a portable JudgmentKit-native UI authority.
priority_rules: If a referenced external design system exists and has a confirmed accessibility baseline or owner-approved review status, that system takes precedence.
If no external design system exists, this pack becomes the source of truth for primitives, tokens, layout archetypes, required states, light-dark theme parity, reusable recipes, and handoff depth.
If the pack, vendored guideline profiles, and brief are all silent about a requirement, escalate instead of inventing a new primitive, vague token, or unsupported interaction.

implementation_model.recipe_format: React+Tailwind snippets with explicit slots, variants, interaction rules, and accessibility API

inventory.label: Layout shell
inventory.description: Top-level application frame with optional rail, header, workspace body, and secondary inspector region.
inventory.usage: Required for app workspace, dashboard, review, and export surfaces.

interaction_rules: Keep the primary task in main and secondary metadata in inspector.
Do not move local controls away from the surface they govern.
accessibility_contract: Use main, nav, header, and aside landmarks where applicable.
Preserve heading order and skip-link destination.

inventory.label: Sidebar or rail
inventory.description: Persistent navigation or collection switcher aligned to the main workspace frame.
inventory.usage: Use for global navigation, queue switching, or mode changes.

interaction_rules: Active item must be visually distinct and keyboard reachable.
Keep mode changes and run history grouped, not interleaved.
accessibility_contract: Use nav with an accessible label.
Use links for navigation and buttons for actions.
component_recipe.react_tailwind: <nav aria-label="Workspace navigation" className="flex flex-col gap-2 rounded-[var(--jk-radius-2)] border border-[var(--jk-color-border-subtle)] bg-[var(--jk-color-surface)] p-4">...</nav>

inventory.label: Header
inventory.description: Surface-level title, status, and primary actions anchored to the region they govern.
inventory.usage: Use for page title, collection metadata, and scoped actions.

interaction_rules: Primary action stays in the same header as the governed surface.
Keep status and secondary metadata subordinate to the title.
accessibility_contract: Use heading tags in hierarchy order.
If actions change nearby content, keep them adjacent in DOM order.
component_recipe.react_tailwind: <header className="flex items-start justify-between gap-4 border-b border-[var(--jk-color-border-subtle)] px-5 py-4">...</header>

inventory.label: Card
inventory.description: Contained surface block with a single content responsibility and explicit header-content-footer structure when needed.
inventory.usage: Use for summaries, settings groups, metrics, and review blocks.

interaction_rules: Use cards to group meaning, not to add decorative nesting.
Promote content hierarchy before adding elevation.
accessibility_contract: Keep heading and body text associated in reading order.
If footer actions exist, ensure they remain within the same card.

inventory.label: Field
inventory.description: Label, control, helper text, and validation message grouped as one primitive.
inventory.usage: Use for text input, toggles, selects, and textarea entry.

interaction_rules: Do not rely on placeholder text as the only label.
Validation copy must stay next to the affected control.
accessibility_contract: Use label or aria-label on every control.
Use aria-describedby for helper and error text when present.

inventory.label: Button
inventory.description: Primary, secondary, or ghost action with explicit hierarchy and nearby scope.
inventory.usage: Use for create, save, continue, approve, and dismiss actions.

interaction_rules: Specific labels beat generic labels.
Destructive actions require confirmation or undo language.
accessibility_contract: Icon-only buttons need aria-label.
Focus-visible state must remain visible.
component_recipe.react_tailwind: <button className="inline-flex min-h-10 items-center justify-center rounded-[var(--jk-radius-2)] px-4 font-medium focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-[var(--jk-color-accent)]">Generate First Pass</button>

inventory.label: Tabs
inventory.description: Mutually exclusive view switcher for peer content regions.
inventory.usage: Use for switching between results, details, code, or history views.

interaction_rules: Tabs are for peer views, not unrelated navigation.
Keep the active trigger visually obvious without decorative chrome.
accessibility_contract: Use tablist, tab, and tabpanel semantics.
Support arrow-key navigation and active-state announcement.
component_recipe.react_tailwind:
...
...

inventory.label: Table or list
inventory.description: Structured repeated item presentation with sortable columns or grouped rows.
inventory.usage: Use for queues, records, events, or artifact history.

interaction_rules: Use table semantics only when column relationships matter.
Long content needs wrap or truncation rules.
accessibility_contract: Use table headers for true tabular relationships.
Lists and checklist rows should expose names and states clearly.

inventory.label: Sheet or drawer
inventory.description: Temporary side panel that preserves context while exposing secondary detail or editing.
inventory.usage: Use for edit-in-place flows, review queues, and scoped secondary tasks.

interaction_rules: Use sheets for secondary context, not the primary workflow.
On compact layouts, preserve overscroll containment.
accessibility_contract: Use dialog semantics with labeled title and close action.
Trap focus while open and restore focus on close.
component_recipe.react_tailwind: ...

inventory.label: Dialog
inventory.description: Blocking confirmation or focused multi-step decision moment with explicit primary and cancel actions.
inventory.usage: Use for destructive confirmation, escalation approval, or constrained review.

interaction_rules: Use dialogs only when the decision should interrupt the current flow.
Keep action labels explicit about the consequence.
accessibility_contract: Use alertdialog or dialog semantics as appropriate.
Provide keyboard dismissal and visible focus management.
component_recipe.react_tailwind: ...

inventory.label: Inspector
inventory.description: Persistent secondary panel for metadata, settings, evidence, or comments tied to the current selection.
inventory.usage: Use when a workspace needs right-side supporting detail.

interaction_rules: Inspector content must support the current selection, not replace the main task.
Keep escalations and ownership next to the evidence they describe.
accessibility_contract: Use aside when the panel is persistent.
Section headings must stay hierarchical and descriptive.
component_recipe.react_tailwind: <aside className="grid gap-4 rounded-[var(--jk-radius-2)] border border-[var(--jk-color-border-subtle)] bg-[var(--jk-color-surface)] p-4">...</aside>

inventory.label: Artifact panel
inventory.description: Inline panel for code, logs, generated artifacts, or previews using the same theme model as the surrounding interface.
inventory.usage: Use for evidence viewers, output previews, and implementation handoff payloads.

interaction_rules: Local controls stay in or directly above the panel.
Artifact surfaces must not adopt a separate dark or light theme.
accessibility_contract: Toolbar actions need clear names and focus order.
Code or preview regions need labels when multiple panels coexist.
component_recipe.react_tailwind:
...
...

composition_rules: Compose only from the published primitive inventory unless the brief explicitly authorizes a new primitive and the change is escalated.
Keep local controls inside or directly adjacent to the surface they govern.
Use cards, fields, artifact panels, and inspectors to express hierarchy instead of decorative wrappers, glass treatments, or ornamental chrome.
Each generated screen must map back to reusable recipes rather than one-off markup fragments.

spacing_scale.usage: compact control groups and small stack gaps
field internals and dense panel spacing

radius_scale.usage: default cards, inputs, drawers, and panels
feature callouts or emphasized containers only

border_rules: Use border emphasis before adding elevation.
Do not exceed the published radius scale or stack multiple ornamental borders.

typography_roles.usage: default body copy and helper text

rules: Keep the primary task in the center workspace and secondary metadata in the inspector.
Do not move local controls out of the workspace header or governed panel.
Group fields into logical cards or sections.
Keep save or continue actions at the end of the affected form region.
Lead with summary signal, then expand into tables, lists, or detail cards.
Evidence and decision controls must stay visually connected.
Decision actions should remain adjacent to the artifact or record being reviewed.
Show fixed implementation commitments before open questions.
Keep export and copy actions next to the payload they affect.
Lead with a single main workspace plus persistent secondary context.
Keep generation, review, and handoff visible in one frame before escalating to extra modes.
Checklist and decision actions stay adjacent to the artifact being reviewed.
Use sheets only for secondary context on compact layouts.
Show implementation commitments before open questions.
Keep export actions next to the payload they affect.

expectations: Show structure-preserving placeholders instead of empty white space.
Keep primary navigation and section labels visible.
Explain what is missing in plain language.
Provide one clear next action in the same region.
Present the main task path without forcing secondary metadata first.
Keep the default state implementation-ready and theme-complete.
Expose the failure clearly without collapsing the rest of the layout.
Provide recovery actions close to the failed region.
Call out the unresolved decision and the owner required.
Keep the escalation signal near the affected surface.
Explain why the action is unavailable.
Do not rely on color alone to communicate the disabled status.

accessibility_notes: Name focus order and keyboard expectations for interactive regions.
Name status semantics for warning, error, and success states.
Name motion-reduction behavior and any assumptions that still need review.
Name any label, aria-live, or dialog semantics required by the recipes.
escalation_triggers: A requested primitive is not in the published inventory.
The brief requires a token, interaction, or state not covered by the pack.
Theme parity cannot be maintained across artifact panels or code surfaces.
The handoff cannot stay concrete without product or design review.
